name: Care Ah Van Express LLC
recent_news: No public news found; small St. Louis NEMT-style operator registered c. 2021. Note: the careavanexpress.com website belongs to a separate Georgia-based NEMT company (Care A Van Express Transportation, founded by Brittany Bennett, serving 11 GA counties), not this MO entity.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~$0.7M rev, ~10 employees, special-needs flag only, no school district flag). Likely NEMT/paratransit micro-operator, not a school bus contractor. Not a Summit tuck-in target.

name: Heart To Heart Transportation
recent_news: No public news, M&A activity, or media coverage found. Small operator in Auburn/Placer County area; no website located.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~$1.3M rev, ~21 employees, single owner-operated). Possible SPED/private school niche operator in Placer County; would need direct outreach to confirm school district contracts and owner succession interest. Marginal tuck-in candidate at best given size.

name: Lincoln Transportation Inc
districts_or_customers: Ruidoso Municipal Schools (likely; co-located in Ruidoso Downs area)
recent_news: ~30-year-old intrastate NM passenger carrier (USDOT 931166, MC 401218); 15 power units, 9 drivers per 2023 MCS-150; intrastate-only, NOT AUTHORIZED for interstate for-hire; no recent M&A or ownership-change news found.
fit_notes: Small (~$1M rev, 15 buses) long-tenured family operator in rural southern NM; likely serves Ruidoso Municipal/Lincoln County schools. Geographically isolated from Summit's core footprint and outside core expansion lanes — only a tuck-in candidate if Summit pursues NM market entry; otherwise too small/remote.

name: Martz Trailways
owner_or_parent: Martz Group (5th-generation family-owned; HQ Wilkes-Barre, PA)
principals: Scott Henry (President, Martz Group, per public records)
districts_or_customers: Maryland Transit Administration (MTA commuter via Gold Line subsidiary)
recent_news: Martz Group remains 5th-generation family-owned (since 1908); operates terminals in PA, MD (Hagerstown), NY, NJ, VA, DC and FL. Subsidiary Gold Line holds MD MTA commuter contracts. No school bus yellow-bus contracting business identified.
fit_notes: NOT a school bus contractor — this is a motorcoach/charter/commuter operator (Trailways affiliate). Employee count (1,734) and revenue ($62.8M) belong to broader Martz Group, not a yellow-bus business. Outside Summit's school-bus tuck-in thesis; exclude as target.

name: Bus Bus & Limoz Limoz
recent_news: No public website, news, or M&A activity located. Houston-area address (3400 Edloe St) is a commercial/office park location; name pattern and service mix strongly suggest charter/limo/party-bus rather than yellow-school-bus contracting.
fit_notes: Name and Houston market positioning point to charter/limo/party-bus rather than contracted home-to-school yellow-bus service. Unverified school flag in source data; very low confidence as a school-bus tuck-in target. Recommend deprioritize unless direct outreach confirms K-12 district contracts.
